profile.name: Justin
profile.extracurriculars_summary: G11 at Old Scona; med-school-track profile combining math/CS elite-competition results, direct clinical exposure, and a founder-level mental-health non-profit. Cohesive narrative linking a grade-9 classmate's mental-health-driven drop-out to his self-directed ML research on at-risk-student identification (IB CS IA).

sources: Harvard MEDScience admission email (2026-04, confirmed acceptance to TECH Foundations Session M3)
extracurriculars.narrative_theme: A grade-9 classmate did not pass the Old Scona entrance exam and dropped out of school within 2 months due to mental-health pressure. Justin researched Alberta youth mental-health trends, combined with his math/ML strengths to self-teach LightGBM/XGBoost, and is now building an ML risk-detection tool for at-risk students as his IB CS IA — 'the tool I wish had existed for my classmate.' Career direction: bioinformatics, bridging clinical practice with data science.

healthcare_exposure.name: Medical Shadowing — Histopathology
healthcare_exposure.org: Dr. Surinder Kanwal, Misericordia Community Hospital, Covenant Health
healthcare_exposure.description: Observed surgical specimen processing, margin evaluation, and lymph-node dissection for cancer diagnosis.

healthcare_exposure.name: Youth Summer Program (MED YSP)
healthcare_exposure.org: University of Toronto Faculty of Medicine
healthcare_exposure.description: Studied disease mechanisms (kidney failure, genetic diseases), neural physiology, and chronic-condition management (cystic fibrosis, obesity).

healthcare_exposure.name: Harvard Medical School MEDscience Summer Program — MEDscienceTECH Foundations
healthcare_exposure.selectivity: Selected from a highly competitive applicant pool (admission letter received 2026-04, before April 15 deadline)
healthcare_exposure.track: MEDscienceTECH Foundations — biomedical engineering + computer science + clinical medicine
healthcare_exposure.description: Hands-on team project at Harvard Medical School building AI computer vision surgical robots on the Kepler AI surgical robotic platform. Students learn the biodesign process and prototype solutions to complex healthcare problems. The week also includes direct clinical exposure: diagnosing and treating simulated patients, learning anatomy and physiology, and getting bedside exposure to careers including surgery. Final activity is a shark-tank-style pitch + live demonstration to medical experts on demo day.
healthcare_exposure.why_it_matters: Sits at the exact intersection of Justin's three core interests: AI / computer vision (his ML work), engineering / robotics (build-from-scratch with the Kepler platform), and clinical medicine (bedside exposure + anatomy/physiology). It's a stronger fit for Waterloo CS than I initially framed — the AIF can cite AI computer vision robotics built in a Harvard cohort. For McMaster/Queen's BHSc, the credential reads as both clinical exposure AND technical capability tied directly to medicine.
healthcare_exposure.notes: Confirmed acceptance; $2,800 program fee due 2026-04-22. Welcome letter scheduled 2026-05-15. Justin's application essay (data/justin/Harvard MEDScience.docx) framed him as the 'Calm Analyst & Team Specialist' — the team-project nature of building robots-in-teams aligns with that framing.

mental_health_focus.name: HelpingMinds.ca (Founder & President)
mental_health_focus.description: Non-profit supporting children's mental health; led fundraising and awareness campaigns partnering with local community groups.

mental_health_focus.name: Chatter Social Skills Program (Volunteer Mentor)
mental_health_focus.description: Mentors neurodiverse children in communication and social skills. 18+ months sustained, 100+ hours of group activities and direct support.

mental_health_focus.name: Community Helpers (Executive Member)
mental_health_focus.description: Trained in suicide prevention and stress management; supports peers at Old Scona.

mathematics: International Mathematics Competition 2024 — Merit Award (Lucknow, India) — represented Canada internationally
Canadian Open Mathematics Challenge (COMC): Distinction 2024, Honours 2023
Canada Lynx Mathematical Competition (CLMC): Honourable Mention (National), Bronze / Silver (Provincial)
University of Waterloo Pascal Contest: Distinction (2023, 2024)
University of Waterloo Fryer Contest: Distinction (2024)
computer_science: CyberPatriot Competition: National Finalist (previous season), Semi-Finalist 2024-25 — Linux specialist
Canadian Computing Competition (CCC): Active competitor (University of Waterloo)
Kaggle / DrivenData: Top 5.7% in 3 ML competitions on health datasets (mental health, vaccine hesitancy); LightGBM / XGBoost

technical_projects.name: Mental Health Risk Prediction Algorithm
technical_projects.description: Web-based survey platform and ML model to identify at-risk high school students from anonymized response patterns — targeting early intervention.

technical_projects.name: Kaggle Titanic ML Retrospective (LinkedIn article)
technical_projects.description: Authored a technical retrospective on feature-engineering vs model complexity trade-offs. Demonstrated that recalibrating the decision threshold from 0.50 to 0.60 moved him ~10,000 places on the leaderboard — showcasing disciplined root-cause debugging over complexity.

leadership.name: Executive Member — Mathematics & Computer Science Club (Old Scona)
leadership.description: Organizes peer-led sessions on Data Structures & Algorithms; facilitates school-wide participation in math and coding contests.

other_achievements: Piano — RCM Level 10 Certificate (classical performance, significant time commitment)
extracurriculars.self_described_team_role: Calm Analyst & Team Specialist — steps back to check foundational assumptions before adding complexity; bridges technical execution with empathy for vulnerable populations.

confirmed.program: Harvard Medical School MEDscience Summer Program — TECH Foundations

summer_2026_plans.notes: Harvard MEDScience is a 1-week program (June 22-26), so it does NOT compete with HYRS — Justin can do both if HYRS accepts him. With Harvard MEDScience confirmed, the formal-clinical-research gap from his earlier profile is partially filled even without HYRS; HYRS now becomes additive (lab research) rather than gap-filling (any clinical exposure).

ec_strengths_by_target.mcmaster_bhsc: VERY STRONG for the written supplementary application. Cohesive narrative (friend → mental health research → ML tools → clinical shadowing → AI in medicine) plus rare histopathology shadowing at Misericordia + UofT MED YSP + Harvard MEDScience MEDscienceTECH Foundations (June 2026, confirmed) + HelpingMinds founder + Chatter 100+ hours + IMC Merit + AP Bio 5 + BIBO Silver. The Harvard MEDScience credential is meaningful: he'll build AI computer vision surgical robots in a Harvard Med team while getting bedside clinical exposure (anatomy, physiology, simulated patient diagnosis & treatment, surgical exposure) — it reinforces both the clinical-exposure pillar AND the AI-meets-medicine narrative McMaster's reflective questions reward. This is exactly the profile McMaster BHSc supplementary readers look for.
ec_strengths_by_target.queens_bhsc: VERY STRONG for the PSE (1 written + 1 video response). Video format well-suited to Chatter mentoring and CyberPatriot team experience. Same narrative arc as McMaster, plus the Harvard MEDScience cohort (June 2026, confirmed) gives him a recent, prestigious team-engineering experience with direct clinical exposure (bedside, anatomy, surgical) to anchor the video response. Depth of sustained mental-health volunteering is distinctive.
ec_strengths_by_target.waterloo_cs: VERY STRONG for the AIF. CyberPatriot National Finalist (Linux), IMC 2024, Pascal/Fryer/CIMC distinctions, CCC, Kaggle top 5.7%, LightGBM/XGBoost, self-built ML projects — exactly what Waterloo CS AIF readers value. **NEW credential to highlight prominently: Harvard Medical School MEDscienceTECH Foundations (June 2026, confirmed) — building AI computer vision surgical robots on the Kepler platform in a team of biomedical engineers, with a shark-tank pitch demo day to medical experts.** This is directly Waterloo-CS-relevant content (AI, computer vision, robotics, team biodesign, prototype-to-pitch) and Justin should lead the AIF with it. GPA is still the bigger gate (Reddit median 97.5), but the AIF case is now materially stronger.
ec_strengths_by_target.uoft_lifesci_stgeorge: NOT APPLICABLE — no supplementary application at UofT Life Sci. ECs do not affect the decision. Justin is well above the academic bar here regardless.
ec_strengths_by_target.tier_2_general: Most Tier-2 programs are academic-record-only and don't read supplementary applications. ECs are irrelevant for admission, but the combination means Justin would be among the top-of-class admits wherever he lands.
watch_items: ELA 30-1 projection 85-90% is the single academic vulnerability — Queen's BHSc requires minimum 80% in ENG4U equivalent, so any slip below 80% is a hard-fail. Otherwise the gap is a softer cap on his essay-writing ceiling.
Physics 30 is not in his course list — check whether any target program requires it (Western BMSc recommends it; Waterloo CS does not; BHSc programs do not).

tier1.supp_app_type: Personal Statement of Experience (PSE) on Kira Talent — 1 timed written response (10 min, 335-word cap) + 1 video response (2 min prep + 2 min delivery)
tier1.supp_app_notes:
Delivered through Kira Talent. Applicants complete the written response
first (10 minutes to write and submit, 335-word hard cap — the FAQ warns
"exceeding the word limit will negatively affect your application"),
then the video response (2 minutes to prepare, 2 minutes to deliver).
Total assessment is ~30 minutes; can be done in one sitting or split
across sessions, but the written piece must be finished before starting
the video.

Mandatory practice questions are part of the check-in process so
applicants can get comfortable with the platform before submitting.
Question text is not released in advance; applicants get questions at
random and re-takes also draw new questions at random.

"Queen's does not accept late submissions." Additional materials
(reference letters, CV, etc.) will not be reviewed. Required for the
on-campus stream. Read by trained committees including reviewers from
Health Sciences, Nursing, and Commerce. Kira Talent does not recommend
third-party browser extensions (e.g. Grammarly) during submission due to
platform compatibility issues.
tier1.decision_window: April through mid-May (rolling; official FAQ commits to communicating all decisions by mid-May)
prereq_courses: Grade 12 Math: one of Advanced Functions (MHF4U) or Calculus & Vectors (MCV4U) — required
tier1.prereq_notes:
Minimum 75% cumulative average required for consideration. Justin's
Alberta equivalents satisfy these: ELA 30-1 (must hit ≥80% — currently
projected 85-90), Biology 30, Chemistry 30, Math 30-1 (Pure Math = AB
Advanced Functions equivalent), Math 31 (= Calculus & Vectors), French
30-1. **The 80% English minimum is the field to watch — Justin's ELA
30-1 projection of 85-90 has cushion but no margin for slipping.**
tier1.reasoning: Justin's projected top-6 midpoint of 95.5% sits at the 36.8th percentile of accepted reports for Queen's University Bachelor of Health Sciences (n=68, median accepted avg 96.4%). Projection range (93.5-97.5%) places him at the 7.4-70.6th percentile. OOP subset (small sample, n=8): mean accepted 96.2%, min 93.5%. Justin's range is consistent with the observed OOP cluster. Supp-app leverage (very high weight at this program): VERY STRONG for the PSE (1 written + 1 video response). Video format well-suited to Chatter mentoring and CyberPatriot team experience. Same narrative arc as McMaster, plus the Harvard MEDScience cohort (June 2026, confirmed) gives him a recent, prestigious team-engineering experience with direct clinical exposure (bedside, anatomy, surgical) to anchor the video response. Depth of sustained mental-health volunteering is distinctive. FINAL VERDICT: TARGET. Caveat: Reddit dataset is heavily self-selected toward acceptances (~95-99% of reports are accepts), so percentiles are relative to admitted students, not raw applicant pools.
tier1.ec_strength_text: VERY STRONG for the PSE (1 written + 1 video response). Video format well-suited to Chatter mentoring and CyberPatriot team experience. Same narrative arc as McMaster, plus the Harvard MEDScience cohort (June 2026, confirmed) gives him a recent, prestigious team-engineering experience with direct clinical exposure (bedside, anatomy, surgical) to anchor the video response. Depth of sustained mental-health volunteering is distinctive.

tier1.oop_caveat: Justin's HIGH projection 97.5% is below the observed OOP floor 97.8% (small sample n=5 — directional only)
tier1.supp_app_type: 2 written essays (1500 characters each, including spaces); choose 1 of 3 prompts for Q1 + 1 of 3 prompts for Q2
tier1.supp_app_notes:
McMaster posts the year's Supp App Questions PDF publicly in September,
so applicants see the prompts months before the portal opens. Applicants
choose 1 of 3 options for Question 1 and 1 of 3 options for Question 2
(so 2 essays drawn from a pool of 6 prompts), with a hard 1500-character
cap per response (including punctuation and spaces). Submission
instructions are emailed in late January, and the online portal is open
for only ~3–4 days in early February (Feb 3–6 in the 2024-25 cycle).
Extensions are not granted.

Prompts are problem-based and philosophical, designed to test how an
applicant thinks — 2025 prompts covered cognitive engagement in the
age of GenAI, Indigenous Seven Grandfather Teachings, interdisciplinary
synergies between health and other fields, and admissions-policy design.

Generative AI is explicitly prohibited: "use of generative AI to write
the response are not permitted, and may result in removal from the
applicant pool." Proofreading tools and human feedback are acceptable
but "the writing itself must be your own work." Posting answers online
or working with others on responses is also disqualifying.

The supp app is the primary differentiator above the ~90% grade floor.
tier1.decision_window: Two waves: early March and early May (first week of each)
prereq_courses: 5 best Grade 12 4U/4M courses including the required English
tier1.prereq_notes:
English 4U is the only formally required prerequisite; Bio + Chem are
recommended. Justin's Alberta equivalents are ELA 30-1, Biology 30,
Chemistry 30 — all satisfied. McMaster will use his top-6 Grade 12
academic average for assessment.
tier1.reasoning: Justin's projected top-6 midpoint of 95.5% sits at the 22.2th percentile of accepted reports for McMaster University Bachelor of Health Sciences (Honours) (n=63, median accepted avg 97.0%). Projection range (93.5-97.5%) places him at the 11.1-69.8th percentile. OOP DIRECTIONAL SIGNAL (small sample, treat with caution): only 5 OOP-accepted reports across 4 cycles, all clustered above 97.8%. Even Justin's HIGH projection of 97.5% is below the observed OOP minimum, but with n=5 this is a YELLOW FLAG, not a hard threshold — one additional data point could shift the floor by several percentage points. Suggests OOP bar may be tougher than in-province; do not let it override the percentile-based verdict. Supp-app leverage (very high weight at this program): VERY STRONG for the written supplementary application. Cohesive narrative (friend → mental health research → ML tools → clinical shadowing → AI in medicine) plus rare histopathology shadowing at Misericordia + UofT MED YSP + Harvard MEDScience MEDscienceTECH Foundations (June 2026, confirmed) + HelpingMinds founder + Chatter 100+ hours + IMC Merit + AP Bio 5 + BIBO Silver. The Harvard MEDScience credential is meaningful: he'll build AI computer vision surgical robots in a Harvard Med team while getting bedside clinical exposure (anatomy, physiology, simulated patient diagnosis & treatment, surgical exposure) — it reinforces both the clinical-exposure pillar AND the AI-meets-medicine narrative McMaster's reflective questions reward. This is exactly the profile McMaster BHSc supplementary readers look for. EC adjustment applied: GPA-only verdict would be REACH, upgraded to TARGET via strong supplementary-application leverage. ECs alone never upgrade a reach to a safety — the upgrade is capped at target. FINAL VERDICT: TARGET. Caveat: Reddit dataset is heavily self-selected toward acceptances (~95-99% of reports are accepts), so percentiles are relative to admitted students, not raw applicant pools.
tier1.ec_strength_text: VERY STRONG for the written supplementary application. Cohesive narrative (friend → mental health research → ML tools → clinical shadowing → AI in medicine) plus rare histopathology shadowing at Misericordia + UofT MED YSP + Harvard MEDScience MEDscienceTECH Foundations (June 2026, confirmed) + HelpingMinds founder + Chatter 100+ hours + IMC Merit + AP Bio 5 + BIBO Silver. The Harvard MEDScience credential is meaningful: he'll build AI computer vision surgical robots in a Harvard Med team while getting bedside clinical exposure (anatomy, physiology, simulated patient diagnosis & treatment, surgical exposure) — it reinforces both the clinical-exposure pillar AND the AI-meets-medicine narrative McMaster's reflective questions reward. This is exactly the profile McMaster BHSc supplementary readers look for.

tier1.supp_app_notes:
The AIF asks about academic background, extracurriculars, programming
experience, contests, and reasons for choosing Waterloo CS. It is a
required part of the application — the program page states
"supplementary information forms, including the Admission Information
Form, are required for admission." Reddit comments frequently cite
hackathon wins, CS contest results (CCC, Euclid, AMC), and
community/competitive programming as common AIF entries.
tier1.decision_window: Mid May (with a deferral round before final decisions)
tier1.prereq_notes:
Recommended: Grade 11 Introduction to Computer Science (ICS3U). For
Alberta students, Math 30-1 maps to Advanced Functions and Math 31
(Calculus) maps to Calculus & Vectors — Justin satisfies both at very
strong levels (99/99). His weakest spot is ELA 30-1 at 87.5 midpoint;
Waterloo doesn't impose an explicit English minimum like Queen's does,
but a higher English mark would help his top-6 average.

CyberPatriot National Finalist (Linux), IMC 2024, Pascal/Fryer/CIMC distinctions, CCC, Kaggle top 5.7%, LightGBM/XGBoost, self-built ML projects — exactly what Waterloo CS AIF readers value. **NEW credential to highlight prominently: Harvard Medical School MEDscienceTECH Foundations (June 2026, confirmed) — building AI computer vision surgical robots on the Kepler platform in a team of biomedical engineers, with a shark-tank pitch demo day to medical experts.** This is directly Waterloo-CS-relevant content (AI, computer vision, robotics, team biodesign, prototype-to-pitch) and Justin should lead the AIF with it. GPA is still the bigger gate (Reddit median 97.5), but the AIF case is now materially stronger. AIF content will be strong but does not change the grade-driven verdict of REACH. FINAL VERDICT: REACH. Caveat: Reddit dataset is heavily self-selected toward acceptances (~95-99% of reports are accepts), so percentiles are relative to admitted students, not raw applicant pools.

tier2.supp_app_notes:
Life Sciences admission category does not require a supplementary
application. Some other A&S programs (Engineering, Music, etc.) do, but
Life Sci is judged on academic record alone.
prereq_courses: English (ELA 30-1 or English 30 for Alberta students)
tier2.prereq_notes:
For Alberta students: ELA 30-1, Math 31 (or AP Calc), plus 3 more
Grade 12 academic courses. Five Grade 12 academic courses minimum.
ELA 30-2 does NOT meet the requirement. Justin satisfies all of these
with room to spare: ELA 30-1 (~87.5), Math 31 (~99), Math 30-1 (~99),
Bio 30 (~97.5), Chem 30 (~97.5), French 30-1 (~92.5).

Important: "challenge subjects, career courses, work experience
courses, and/or co-op courses" are excluded from consideration.
Justin's six listed courses are all academic, so this isn't a problem.
tier2.reasoning: Justin's projected top-6 midpoint of 95.5% sits at the 65.3th percentile of accepted reports for University of Toronto (St. George) Life Sciences (n=150, median accepted avg 94.2%). Projection range (93.5-97.5%) places him at the 40.0-86.0th percentile. Out-of-province subset (n=10): mean accepted 95.1%, min 89.0%. No adverse OOP signal for Justin's projection range. This program does not use a supplementary application; ECs do not affect the admission decision. Verdict is driven entirely by GPA percentile. FINAL VERDICT: SAFETY. Caveat: Reddit dataset is heavily self-selected toward acceptances (~95-99% of reports are accepts), so percentiles are relative to admitted students, not raw applicant pools.

No specific science prerequisites at first-year entry — students can
choose Health Sciences without Bio/Chem/Math at this stage. Some Year 2
specializations (Nursing, Kinesiology) have additional prereqs and
higher cutoffs.
tier3.reasoning: Justin's projected top-6 midpoint of 95.5% sits at the 61.9th percentile of accepted reports for Western University Health Sciences (BHSc) (n=63, median accepted avg 94.7%). Projection range (93.5-97.5%) places him at the 31.7-92.1th percentile. OOP subset (small sample, n=6): mean accepted 94.5%, min 90.0%. Justin's range is consistent with the observed OOP cluster. This program does not use a supplementary application; ECs do not affect the admission decision. Verdict is driven entirely by GPA percentile. FINAL VERDICT: TARGET. Caveat: Reddit dataset is heavily self-selected toward acceptances (~95-99% of reports are accepts), so percentiles are relative to admitted students, not raw applicant pools.

caveats.label: Self-selection bias
caveats.body: Reddit users overwhelmingly post when they're accepted (~93-99% of decisions reported are acceptances). Treat all 'accepted average' numbers as the FLOOR of what gets in, not as a 50/50 cutoff line. Justin's percentile is relative to admitted students, not raw applicant pools.

caveats.label: OOP small samples
caveats.body: Out-of-province sample sizes are 4-12 reports per program. The verdict model treats OOP floors as directional only when n_oop < 10 — small-sample minimums are too noisy to override percentile-based verdicts.

caveats.label: McMaster BHSc OOP yellow flag
caveats.body: Only 5 OOP-accepted reports for McMaster BHSc across 4 cycles, all with averages ≥ 97.8%. The verdict model surfaces this as directional rather than verdict-changing, but it suggests the OOP bar may be meaningfully higher than in-province. Justin should know this.

caveats.label: Schema drift across cycles
caveats.body: The 4 Reddit forms used different question sets across years. The normalizer maps everything to a canonical 'best_avg' field. See workflows/clean_application_data.md for details.

caveats.label: 2025-26 cycle still in progress
caveats.body: The 2025-2026 cycles (Ontario + Alberta) are still receiving submissions through ~end of May 2026. Numbers from that cycle are provisional and skew toward earlier-cycle decisions.

caveats.label: Tier-2 requirements confidence: medium
caveats.body: All 9 Tier-2 requirement entries are confidence=medium. Prerequisite courses are high-confidence (well-known and stable); specific dates default to the universal Jan 15 OUAC date and competitive averages are educated guesses anchored to Reddit data.

caveats.label: Tier-3/4 not curated
caveats.body: Tier-3 (Waterloo CS variants) and Tier-4 (UAlberta) program requirements have NOT been curated. Their checklist rows are blank.

caveats.label: Dates are shifted +1 year from cycle template
caveats.body: Curated requirements use the 2025-2026 cycle as a template. The dashboard shifts every date by +365 days to reflect Justin's actual application cycle (2026-2027). OUAC and supp deadlines may shift by ±1 day in the actual cycle — verify against official sources before relying on exact dates.
